title: Getting Started - pullDB Help
description: Get started with pullDB in minutes — install the CLI, authenticate, and run your first database restore.
headings: Find Your Customer and List Backups
content: Skip to main content Navigation Getting Started CLI API Concepts Web UI Search documentation ESC Help Getting Started Getting Started with pullDB Get from zero to your first restored database in under 5 minutes. This guide covers everything you need to start using pullDB. ~5 minutes 1 Prerequisites Before you start, make sure you have: Access to a machine with pulldb installed A valid username registered in the system Knowledge of at least one customer ID with available backups Not sure about yo

title: CLI Reference - pullDB Help
description: pullDB CLI command reference — restore, search, list, cancel, admin commands with full syntax and examples.
content: Skip to main content Navigation Getting Started CLI API Concepts Web UI Search documentation ESC Help CLI Reference CLI Reference Complete reference for pullDB command-line tools. The pulldb command is for end users to submit and monitor restore jobs. The pulldb-admin command is for system administrators. Quick Reference Quick Reference # Submit a restore $ pulldb restore <customer> # Check your last job status $ pulldb status # Stream events in real-time $ pulldb status --rt # Cancel a job $ pu

title: API Reference - pullDB Help
description: pullDB REST API reference — HMAC authentication, endpoints, request/response examples.
content: Skip to main content Navigation Getting Started CLI API Concepts Web UI Search documentation ESC Help API Reference API Reference pullDB provides a RESTful API for programmatic access. The API uses JSON for request and response bodies. Most users will use the CLI, but the API is available for integration into other tools. Documentation Scope This page covers the most commonly used API endpoints for CLI integration. The full API includes 53 endpoints. For complete coverage including admin APIs, s

title: Web UI Overview - pullDB Help
description: pullDB Web UI guide — navigate the dashboard, jobs, restore form, admin panel, and user profile.
content: Skip to main content Navigation Getting Started CLI API Concepts Web UI Search documentation ESC Help Web UI Overview Web UI Overview The pullDB Web UI provides a modern, browser-based interface for managing database restores. Submit jobs, monitor progress, manage API keys, and administer the system—all from your browser. Overview The Web UI offers the same functionality as the CLI but with a visual interface that makes it easy to monitor multiple jobs, browse backup history, and manage your acc

title: Dashboard - pullDB Help
description: pullDB Dashboard — view active jobs, recent activity, team statistics, and system status.
content: Skip to main content Navigation Getting Started CLI API Concepts Web UI Search documentation ESC Help Web UI Dashboard Dashboard The Dashboard is your home base in pullDB. It provides an at-a-glance view of your jobs, system status, and quick actions tailored to your role. Overview What you see on the Dashboard depends on your role. Users with the user role see their own job activity, manager users see system-wide metrics, and admin users get full access to administration panels. Dashboard featu

title: Jobs Management - pullDB Help
description: pullDB Jobs page — monitor restore jobs, view status, connection info, and manage job lifecycle.
content: Skip to main content Navigation Getting Started CLI API Concepts Web UI Search documentation ESC Help Web UI Jobs Management Jobs Management The Jobs page lets you view, monitor, and manage your restore jobs. Track progress in real-time and view detailed logs for each job. Overview The Jobs page provides a comprehensive view of all your restore jobs. You can see active jobs updating in real-time, browse job history, and drill down into details. Job List Sortable, filterable table of all jobs. Se

title: Restore Workflow - pullDB Help
description: pullDB Restore form — select a customer database, configure target settings, and start a restore job.
headings: What to Expect on the Job Detail Page
Leaving the target as auto-generated when you need a specific name
Enabling Overwrite on a database you are still using
Backup list is empty after selecting a customer
content: Skip to main content Navigation Getting Started CLI API Concepts Web UI Search documentation ESC Help Web UI Restore Workflow Restore Workflow The restore page is a single-page form at /web/restore . It has two numbered sections: Source Selection and Target Configuration. Fill them in order and click Queue Restore to submit the job. ~1 minute to submit Overview Click New Restore in the sidebar to open the form. The page is divided into two sections that you complete from top to bottom. There is

description: pullDB User Profile — manage API keys, change password, and control your deployed databases.
content: Skip to main content Navigation Getting Started CLI API Concepts Web UI Search documentation ESC Help Web UI Profile & API Keys Profile & API Keys Manage your account settings, change your password, and create API keys for programmatic access to pullDB from scripts and automation tools. Overview The Profile page ( /web/auth/profile ) is your personal settings area. Here you can view your account information, change your password, set your default host, and manage API keys for each host you have

Managers can view team statistics, track active job counts, and reset team member passwords when needed. Access it from the sidebar under "Resources". All Users Welcome Feature Requests is available to all authenticated users. description: pullDB job lifecycle — understand all 11 job states from queued to deleted with transition rules.
content: Skip to main content Navigation Getting Started CLI API Concepts Web UI Search documentation ESC Help Concepts Job Lifecycle Job Lifecycle Understanding how restore jobs move through the system from submission to completion. This knowledge helps you monitor progress, diagnose issues, and understand what's happening behind the scenes. Overview When you submit a restore job, it goes through a series of states and phases . Can't find what you're looking for? Use the search or check the CLI reference .
